evals.prompt: Set up a new Python project called dataloader with modern tooling: linting, formatting, type checking, and tests.
evals.expected_output: A project skeleton with pyproject.toml (PEP 621 metadata), ruff for lint+format, mypy or pyright config, pytest layout, and pre-commit hooks.
expectations: pyproject.toml contains PEP 621 metadata and tool configs
ruff is configured for linting and formatting
tests/ layout works with pytest out of the box
pre-commit config wires the tools together

evals.prompt: Migrate this setup.py project to pyproject.toml without breaking the existing CLI entry point.
evals.expected_output: A pyproject.toml carrying over metadata, dependencies, and console scripts, with the migration steps and verification commands.
expectations: Console script entry point is preserved in [project.scripts]

evals.prompt: Add strict type checking and pre-commit hooks to our existing Python repo gradually, without blocking the team.
evals.expected_output: An incremental adoption plan: baseline mypy config with per-module strictness, pre-commit setup, and CI wiring that doesn't fail on legacy code.
expectations: Type checking is introduced incrementally (per-module or baseline)
Plan explicitly avoids blocking existing development
